The model number will most likely appear on either a paper sticker or a metal plate. Start Shopping ![to Canada site](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/images/canada-flag.png) PartSelect.ca [Full Details](https://www.partselect.com/About/History/EAP/) [REPAIR](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/) \> [REFRIGERATOR](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/) \> FRIDGE TOO COLD # How To Fix A Refrigerator That's Too Cold ![How To Fix A Refrigerator That's Too Cold](https://img.youtube.com/vi/t67G2YUuyWs/hqdefault.jpg) ### Click a Part Below to Start Your Repair: [Air Inlet Damper Or Baffle](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/Refrigerator-Too-Cold/#AirInletDamper) [Temperature Sensor Or Thermistor](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/Refrigerator-Too-Cold/#TemperatureSensorOr) [Main Control Board](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/Refrigerator-Too-Cold/#MainControl) [Cold Control Or Temperature Control](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/Refrigerator-Too-Cold/#ColdControlOr) ### About this repair: - Rated as REALLY EASY - 257 repair stories - 2 step by step videos ## Air Inlet Damper Or Baffle The air damper, baffle or diffuser is the device that balances the air flow from the evaporator fan housing. This normally is a mechanically controlled baffle or flapper that controls the amount of cold air entering the fresh food compartment. If the baffle is damaged or the linkage to the control knob is damaged, then too much cold air may enter the fresh food compartment resulting in lower than normal temperatures. The baffle or damper will be located where the cold air enters the fresh food compartment. The housing that the baffle or damper is encased in is often made of plastic with a Styrofoam lining and may have a foam seal to prevent air leakage. **How to inspect a refrigerator’s air inlet damper or baffle:** 1. Unplug your refrigerator and locate the damper or baffle. It is usually found where the cold air enters the fresh food compartment. 2. Use caution when inspecting the baffle or damper to not damage the plastic & Styrofoam housing that the baffle or damper is encased in. 3. If your model of has a temperature sensing bulb attached to this control make sure that the sensing bulb is in the correct position and not damaged. 4. The control knob should move freely, try to move it to verify that the linkage is intact and that it moves freely. 5. If the damper/baffle is stuck in the closed or nearly closed position or does not move freely, then you need a [replacement air inlet damper or baffle](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Ducts-and-Vents.htm "OEM Replacement Refrigerator Ducts & Vents"). Repair guide: refrigerator freezer too warm. Appliance: refrigerator. Symptoms: refrigerator freezer too warm. Here [View All Refrigerator Parts](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Parts.htm) ![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)![](<Base64-Image-Removed>) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Evaporator Fan Motor The evaporator fan motor is used to circulate the cold air throughout the refrigerator when the compressor is running. If the fan is defective, then temperatures in the freezer section will rise slowly and temperatures in the fresh food section will rise more rapidly and the compressor will run longer and more frequently than normal. With the compressor running you should also hear the evaporator fan running at the same time, as well as the condenser fan which is located next to the compressor. **How to inspect a refrigerator evaporator fan motor:** 1. Disconnect your refrigerator from the power source and remove the evaporator fan cover, which will be found in the freezer compartment. 2. The motor shaft should turn freely with minimal resistance, attempt to turn the shaft manually to check for any resistance. 3. If there is a significant amount of ice on the motor, defrost your freezer and see if that resolves the issue. You should also inspect the motor for any signs of wear or damage. 4. If you cannot turn it easily, or if it is noticeably worn, you need a [replacement evaporator fan motor](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Motors.htm "OEM Replacement Refrigerator Motors"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Refrigerator Motors](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Motors.htm) ![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)![](<Base64-Image-Removed>) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Electronic Control Board Some newer models of refrigerators may use an electronic control board. Thermistors or temperature sensors are connected to the control board and are used to monitor temperatures in the fresh food and freezer compartments. The control board uses this information to control the operation of the compressor, fan motors and defrost system. If the temperatures are too warm, then the electronic control could be at fault. Electronic controls are complex and expensive and usually reliable, so you should first verify that all of the other components such as the compressor, fans and sensors are operating properly before condemning the control. Some manufacturers may provide specific information that will help in diagnosing a defective control. **How to inspect your refrigerator’s electronic control board:** 1. Unplug your fridge before beginning as you will be handling electrical components. 2. Locate and remove the electronic control board from your appliance. It is inside of the refrigerator, inside of the control box, which is usually found attached to the top or side of the fridge. 3. Inspect your control board for any signs of damaged foil, wear, burnt connections, arcing, or any other damage. 4. If you find any of the above, you need a [replacement electronic control board](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Circuit-Boards-and-Touch-Pads.htm "OEM Replacement Refrigerator Electronic Boards"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Refrigerator Circuit Boards and Touch Pads](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Circuit-Boards-and-Touch-Pads.htm) ![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)![](<Base64-Image-Removed>)![](<Base64-Image-Removed>) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Temperature Sensor Or Thermistor In models that use an Electronic Control, a thermistor or temperature sensor may be used to monitor fresh food and freezer temperatures. The sensor is a

Repair guide: refrigerator too cold. Appliance: refrigerator. Symptoms: refrigerator too cold. Refrigerator Ducts & Vents"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Refrigerator Parts](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Parts.htm) ![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/966602_2_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/966602_4_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/966602_3_N.JPG) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Temperature Sensor Or Thermistor Some electronic controls models will use a thermistor to sense the temperature. The electronic control monitors this temperature and will automatically adjust the damper. The temperature sensor is normally a plastic encased capsule with two wires attached and may be located near the air inlet. The thermistor will have a specific resistance based on temperature and is therefore difficult to diagnose without the manufacturer’s specifications. Some models may indicate a fault code when the sensor is faulty. **How to test a refrigerator temperature sensor or thermistor with a multi-meter:** 1. Verify that you have disconnected your appliance from the power source as you will be inspecting electrical components. 2. The thermistor(s) will be connected to the main control board, you will need to remove the back panel of your fridge to access the control board. Remove the thermistor(s) from the appliance in order to test them. 3. Thermistor(s) will produce different multi-meter readings based on their temperature when you test them. The ranges below only apply to thermistors at 32 degrees F. Before testing them, you will need to place the thermistor(s) in ice water for 5 minutes to bring the temperature to 32 degrees F. 4. Set your multi-meter to the Rx1 setting and touch the probes to the terminals, you should receive a reading of 16,600 ohms (+/- 5 percent). 5. If you receive a reading outside this range, you will need a [replacement temperature sensor or thermistor](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Sensors.htm "OEM Replacement Refrigerator Sensors"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Refrigerator Sensors](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Sensors.htm) ![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/304103_2_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/968736_1_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/304103_1_N.JPG) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Main Control Board Some newer models may have an electronic control board that controls all of the refrigerators functions. Sensors are connected to the board that monitor fresh food and freezer compartment temperatures and the control board will use that information to operate the compressor and fans as well as the defrost system and in some cases the air baffle/damper to balance temperatures. A defective control board could cause a symptom of the fresh food compartment being too cold, but you should first eliminate the sensors and the damper before changing the control. Some manufacturers may provide detailed information on the procedure for testing the board and the sensors. **How to inspect a refrigerator’s main control board:** 1. Disconnect your refrigerator from the power source, you will be inspecting electrical components. 2. The main control board is usually found inside the control box, which is inside the fridge, attached to the top or side of the fridge. Locate and remove the main control board from your appliance. 3. Looking for any signs of damaged foil, wear, burnt connections, arcing, or any other damage, inspect your main control board. 4. If you find any of the above, you need a [replacement main control board](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Circuit-Boards-and-Touch-Pads.htm "OEM Replacement Refrigerator Electronic Boards").

This normally is a mechanically controlled baffle or flapper that controls the amount of cold air entering the fresh food compartment. If the baffle is damaged or the linkage to the control knob is damaged, then you may not have enough cold air entering the fresh food compartment resulting in higher than normal temperatures. **How to inspect a refrigerator’s air inlet damper or baffle:** 1. Disconnect your refrigerator from the power source and open your refrigerator door to locate the damper or baffle. It will be found where the cold air enters the fresh food compartment. 2. The housing that the baffle or damper is encased in is often made of plastic with a Styrofoam lining and may have a foam seal to prevent air leakage. Care should be used when inspecting the housing to avoid damage to the seal or the Styrofoam. 3. Some refrigerator models use a temperature sensing bulb attached to this control that will automatically adjust the control for changes in the interior temperature. Make sure that the sensing bulb is in the correct position and is not damaged. 4. Attempt to operate the control knob to verify that the linkage is intact and that it moves freely. 5. If the damper/baffle is stuck in the closed or nearly closed position or does not move freely, then you need a [replacement air inlet damper or baffle](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Ducts-and-Vents.htm "OEM Replacement Refrigerator Ducts & Vents"). Start Shopping ![to Canada site](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/images/canada-flag.png) PartSelect.ca [Full Details](https://www.partselect.com/About/History/EAP/) [REPAIR](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/) \> [REFRIGERATOR](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/) \> WILL NOT START # How To Fix A Refrigerator That Will Not Start ![How To Fix A Refrigerator That Will Not Start](<Base64-Image-Removed>) ### Click a Part Below to Start Your Repair: [Overload Or Relay-Start Capacitor](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/Will-Not-Start/#OverloadOrRelay-Start) [Cold Control & Temperature Control](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/Will-Not-Start/#ColdControlAnd) [Electronic Control Board](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Refrigerator/Will-Not-Start/#ElectronicControl) ### About this repair: - Rated as EASY - 365 repair stories - 8 step by step videos ## Overload Or Relay-Start Capacitor The overload relay is a protection device used in the compressor circuit on your refrigerator. Power is applied to the compressor motor windings through the overload device, and the relay is used to add the start winding in the circuit until the compressor is at running speed. If the fans are running and your compressor won’t start or if you hear a clicking sound from the unit, check the overload relay for signs of overheating or arcing. **How to test a refrigerator’s overload or relay-start capacitor with a multi-meter:** 1. Begin by ensuring you have unplugged your refrigerator, and then remove the rear panel. 2. Locate and remove your overload or relay-start capacitor. On modern refrigerators the overload relay is usually a combined part and plugs directly onto the side of the compressor. There may also be a start capacitor attached to the relay overload assembly, which provides increased starting voltage to the compressor windings. 3. To test, begin by discharging the capacitor, then set your multi-meter to the Rx1 setting and place the probes onto the terminals of the overload capacitor and/or the relay-start capacitor. The reading you should receive will vary from model to model, and may be indicated on the outside of the capacitor, or in your owner’s manual. 4. If your test does not produce the reading the manufacturer recommends, you need a [replacement overload or relay-start capacitor](https://www.partselect.com/Refrigerator-Compressors.htm "OEM Replacement Refrigerator Compressors"). If your tests do produce the recommended readings you may have a defective compressor, which will need to be tested by a qualified professional as it is a live circuit test. samples.response:
For a refrigerator that is not cooling, you may want to check these components:

- **Evaporator Fan Motor:**
    - Disconnect power and remove the evaporator fan cover in the freezer.
    - The motor shaft should turn freely; check for resistance.
    - Defrost if there's ice, and inspect for wear or damage.
    - Replace if it doesn't turn easily or is worn.
- **Electronic Control Board:**
    - Unplug the fridge and locate the control board.
    - Inspect for damaged foil, wear, burnt connections, or arcing.
    - Replace if any damage is found.
samples.reference: A refrigerator that isn't cooling is commonly caused by a failed evaporator fan motor, dirty condenser coils, a faulty start relay, or a defective temperature/cold control thermostat. Check that the condenser coils are clean and the evaporator fan runs; replace the failed component if found.

retrieved_contexts: Repair guide: not draining. Appliance: dishwasher. Symptoms: not draining. there is any obstruction to the input and output of the pump and also verify that there is power getting to the pump motor during the drain portion of the cycle. **How to test a dishwasher pump with a multimeter:** 1. Before beginning, ensure that you have disconnected your appliance from the power source. 2. Locate and remove your dishwasher pump. It likely will be found behind the lower access panel. 3. Once you have removed it, using a multimeter on Rx1 mode, touch the pump’s terminals with the probes. You are testing for continuity and should receive a reading of zero or nearly zero. Next, you should test the ground connection by touching one of the probes to the bare metal housing while leaving one on a terminal. This test should not produce any reading. 4. If the results of your tests are different from the above, you will need a [replacement pump](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Pumps.htm "OEM Replacement Dishwasher Pumps"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Dishwasher Pumps](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Pumps.htm) ![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/1156759_1_N.jpg)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/2358460_1_N.jpg)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/1021347_1_N.jpg) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) [How to test a motor](https://www.partselect.com/dishwasher+test-motor+repair.htm) [How to replace a motor](https://www.partselect.com/dishwasher+replace-motor+repair.htm) Back to Top ## Check Valve Flapper Some dishwasher models will use a check valve in the drain outlet. The check valve is used to allow water to flow in one direction but will prevent the dirty water from re-entering the dishwasher. If you suspect that the drain hose has a restriction or if you find that wastewater is getting back into the dishwasher tub, then the check valve is most likely the problem. **How to inspect the check valve flapper in a dishwasher:** 1. To ensure your safety, unplug your dishwasher before beginning this inspection. 2. Locate your check valve flapper in order to inspect it. In most models, it will be found on the outlet port of the drain pump or housing. You will need to remove the lower access panel in order to inspect it. 3. Visually inspect the flapper for any signs of wear or damage. You should also check to see if there is any dirt or debris preventing the flapper from moving freely, remove anything you may find. 4. If your valve is damaged, or if you find no objects preventing it from opening properly, you will need a [replacement check valve flapper](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Parts.htm "OEM Replacement Dishwasher Parts"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Dishwasher Parts](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Parts.htm) ![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/258169_1_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/2099292_1_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/258167_1_N.JPG) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Belt Some older dishwashers may use a belt driven pump. If the belt has come off or is slipping, then the pump that drains the dishwasher won’t function properly. **How to inspect a dishwasher belt:** 1. Similar to other dishwasher inspections, disconnect your appliance from the power source before beginning. 2. Your dishwasher belt will be found behind the lower access panel, attached to a pulley on the pump assembly and to another pulley on the motor assembly. Remove the belt in order to closely inspect it. 3. Looking for any signs of

Repair guide: not draining. Appliance: dishwasher. Symptoms: not draining. appliance from the power source before beginning. 2. Your dishwasher belt will be found behind the lower access panel, attached to a pulley on the pump assembly and to another pulley on the motor assembly. Remove the belt in order to closely inspect it. 3. Looking for any signs of burning, stretching, cracking, damage, or wear, inspect the belt. 4. If you find any issues while inspecting it, you will need a [replacement belt](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Parts.htm "OEM Replacement Dishwasher Parts"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Dishwasher Parts](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Parts.htm) ![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/346995_1_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/1617491_1_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/346995_3_N.JPG) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Timer Some dishwashers will use a mechanical timer to operate the cycles. The timer controls the main pump motor as well as the drain solenoid or separate drain pump motor if your model has that style. The timer is normally located in the control panel at the top of the dishwasher door. You will require a wiring diagram and schematic to identify the correct timer contacts that control the drain cycle. These can then be checked for continuity with a multi-meter and if defective then the timer will need to be replaced. **How to test a dishwasher timer with a multimeter:** 1. Unplug your dishwasher before beginning this test. 2. Your timer will likely be found either in the control panel or behind the lower kickplate. Locate and remove your appliance’s timer in order to test it. 3. If your model has multiple sets of contacts/wires, you will need to refer to your owner’s manual to determine which sets to test. 4. Remove the timer from your appliance, set your multimeter to Rx1000, and touch the contacts/wires with the probes. In most models, a functional timer will produce a reading of 2000-3500 ohms of resistance, but this varies from model to model. Please refer to your owner's manual to determine what reading you should see. 5. If the results of your test are different than the manufacturer’s guidelines, you will need a [replacement timer](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Timers.htm "OEM Replacement Dishwasher Timers"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Dishwasher Timers](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Timers.htm) ![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/2099805_1_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/959249_4_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/260113_3_N.JPG) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) [How to test a timer](https://www.partselect.com/dishwasher+test-timer+repair.htm) [How to replace a timer](https://www.partselect.com/dishwasher+replace-timer+repair.htm) Back to Top ## Drain Hose A dishwasher will not drain properly if it has a restricted or clogged drain hose. Restrictions typically appear most often at the outlet from the pump or drain housing where a check valve may be located, at the input to the household drain system or anywhere that a kink may have formed in the drain hose. If food debris has caused a restriction, then you should check the condition of the food chopper as a possible source of the problem. **How to inspect a dishwasher drain hose:** 1. Verify that the water and power sources have been disconnected from your dishwasher before beginning this inspection. 2. Remove the lower access panel of your appliance and locate the drain. It will be connected to a pump below the lower spray arm. 3. Remove

Start Shopping ![to Canada site](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/images/canada-flag.png) PartSelect.ca [Full Details](https://www.partselect.com/About/History/EAP/) [REPAIR](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/) \> [DISHWASHER](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Dishwasher/) \> NOT DRAINING # How To Fix A Dishwasher That Won't Drain ![How To Fix A Dishwasher That Won't Drain](https://img.youtube.com/vi/9DqYkzF0qVM/hqdefault.jpg) ### Click a Part Below to Start Your Repair: [Piston & Nut Assembly](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Dishwasher/Not-Draining/#PistonAndNut) [Drain Pump & Motor](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Dishwasher/Not-Draining/#DrainPumpAnd) [Check Valve Flapper](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Dishwasher/Not-Draining/#CheckValve) [Belt](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Dishwasher/Not-Draining/#Belt) [Timer](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Dishwasher/Not-Draining/#Timer) [Drain Hose](https://www.partselect.com/Repair/Dishwasher/Not-Draining/#DrainHose) ### About this repair: - Rated as EASY - 564 repair stories - 7 step by step videos ## Piston & Nut Assembly Some models of dishwashers utilize a check valve as part of the drain pump. Within the check valve, there is the piston and nut assembly. If your dishwasher is not draining water properly, the piston and nut assembly could be the source of the problem. **How to inspect a dishwasher piston & nut assembly:** 1. Disconnect your appliance from the power source. 2. Locate your piston & nut assembly. It likely will be found on the bottom of the tub, beneath the sump cover. 3. Inspect your piston & nut assembly to determine if it is functioning properly. The assembly should move up and down freely. When the piston is in the down position, it should form a tight seal. If it is not forming a complete seal, the dishwasher will not completely pump out the water. 4. If you find any issues while performing the inspection, you will need a [replacement piston & nut assembly](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Parts.htm "OEM Replacement Dishwasher Parts"). Start Your Repair Here [View All Dishwasher Parts](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Parts.htm) ![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/260690_1_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/260690_4_N.JPG)![](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/assets/partimages/260690_2_N.JPG) SEARCH [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Back to Top ## Drain Pump & Motor All dishwashers will have a method to drain the water. Most models will have a single motor driven pump with two separate compartments, one for circulation and one for drain, each with its own impeller. Other types will have a separate drain pump, and some will utilize the main circulating pump in conjunction with a drain solenoid and diverter valve or flapper. On models that use a separate drain pump, you should check to see if there is any obstruction to the input and output of the pump and also verify that there is power getting to the pump motor during the drain portion of the cycle. **How to test a dishwasher pump with a multimeter:** 1. Before beginning, ensure that you have disconnected your appliance

Repair guide: not draining. Appliance: dishwasher. Symptoms: not draining. inspect a dishwasher drain hose:** 1. Verify that the water and power sources have been disconnected from your dishwasher before beginning this inspection. 2. Remove the lower access panel of your appliance and locate the drain. It will be connected to a pump below the lower spray arm. 3. Remove the hose in order to inspect the drain hose closely. You are looking for any visible signs of cracking, damage or wear. You should also attempt to run water through the hose to determine if there is a blockage inside. 4. If any issues are found while doing the inspections above, you will need a [replacement hose](https://www.partselect.com/Dishwasher-Hoses-and-Tubes.htm "OEM Replacement Dishwasher Hoses"). samples.response:
To troubleshoot a dishwasher that won't drain, consider these steps:
- Check for any obstruction to the pump's input and output.
- Verify that power is getting to the pump motor during the drain cycle.
- **Inspect the drain hose:** Look for restrictions, clogs, or kinks.
- **Inspect the check valve flapper:** Check for wear, damage, dirt, or debris preventing free movement.
- **Test the dishwasher pump:** Disconnect power, remove the pump, and use a multimeter on Rx1 mode. Touch the pump’s terminals; you should get a reading of zero or nearly zero. Test the ground connection by touching one probe to the bare metal housing and one to a terminal; this should not produce a reading.
samples.reference: A dishwasher that won't drain is commonly caused by a clogged drain filter or hose, a failed drain pump, or a faulty check valve. Clean the filter and hose; if water still remains, replace the drain pump.

The water inlet valve is located at the back of the refrigerator, and connects to the water supply. The valve can break from material fatigue or normal use. If your water inlet valve is malfunctioning, you could be getting a lack of water to the inside of your refrigerator. Your ice maker may not produce cubes or your water dispenser may not work. The water inlet valve must be replaced if the solenoids no longer have continuity or the valve has internal blockage. This valve measures approximately 7 inches long and 4.5 inches wide, and is constructed of plastic and metal. It comes in blue, and includes the valve and mounting bracket. ![How Buying OEM Parts Can Save You Time and Money](<Base64-Image-Removed>) How Buying OEM Parts Can Save You Time and Money Why buy the real thing? OEM stands for Original Equipment Manufacturer. Part: Refrigerator Water Inlet Valve. Part Number: PS7784018. Category: dishwasher. Type: . Brand: . Fixes: leaking, ice maker not making ice, not dispensing water, ice maker won't dispense ice, ice maker dispenses too little ice, leaks water. Compatible models: AP5671757, 2692163, 242252702, 240321801, 240505101, 240505102, 240508101, 240508102, 240531101, 241734301. water valve. You can use a wrench to loosen the flare nut. Your water inlet valve is held in place on your refrigerator by means of a metal bracket. Use a screwdriver or a nutdriver to remove the screw that holds the water valve's bracket to the refrigerator cabinet. Gently pull the water valve out of the cabinet, and remove the tube from the outlet port. There are two wires connecting to the terminals of your water inlet valve. If yours is a double solenoid water valve, it will have four wires. Be sure to label each wire so that you know where to reconnect them later. Firmly grasp the metal connector of the wires in order to remove the wires. You may need to use a pair of needle-nosed pliers. Do not pull on the wires themselves. Set your multitester to the R X 1 ohms setting to test for continuity. Place each of the multitester's leads on a terminal. Your multitester should display a reading between 200 and 500 ohms. Closely examine your water inlet valve. The exact ohm resistance rating you should test for may be marked on the valve. If the test you conduct with your icemaker water inlet valve does not produce these results, then you should replace the component. I hope this helps. Thank you and have a great day! 86 people found this helpful. Did this question help you? Thank you for voting! I hope this helps. 48 people found this helpful. Did this question help you? Thank you for voting! Jacques October 10, 2017 How to test ice water valve or verified ice water valve if its good. [Need help finding your model number?](https://www.partselect.com/Find-Your-Model-Number/) Find PartSelect Number PS7784017 Manufacturer Part Number 242252603 Manufactured by Frigidaire for Frigidaire, Kenmore, Electrolux, Crosley Jump to: - [Product Description](https://www.partselect.com/PS7784017-Frigidaire-242252603-Water-Inlet-Valve.htm#ProductDescription) - [Related Parts](https://www.partselect.com/PS7784017-Frigidaire-242252603-Water-Inlet-Valve.htm#RelatedParts) - [Troubleshooting](https://www.partselect.com/PS7784017-Frigidaire-242252603-Water-Inlet-Valve.htm#Troubleshooting) - [Customer Reviews](https://www.partselect.com/PS7784017-Frigidaire-242252603-Water-Inlet-Valve.htm#CustomerReviews) - [Installation Instructions](https://www.partselect.com/PS7784017-Frigidaire-242252603-Water-Inlet-Valve.htm#InstallationInstructions) - [Questions and Answers](https://www.partselect.com/PS7784017-Frigidaire-242252603-Water-Inlet-Valve.htm#QuestionsAndAnswers) - [Model Cross Reference](https://www.partselect.com/PS7784017-Frigidaire-242252603-Water-Inlet-Valve.htm#ModelCrossReference) Product Description ## Water Inlet Valve Specifications If you notice that the ice maker in your refrigerator is leaking or is not making or dispensing ice, you may need to replace the water inlet valve. When energized, the water inlet valve releases water from the supply line into the ice mold. The water inlet valve is in the back of your refrigerator so you will need to pull the fridge out from the wall before completing this repair. To complete this job, you will need a 1/2 inch wrench, a 1/4 inch nut driver and a utility knife. If your old inlet valve has a screw-in fitting, you may need to simply cut the end of the hose to fit in the new part. Be sure to disconnect both the water and power supply from the refrigerator prior to beginning this repair. ![How Buying OEM Parts Can Save You Time and Money](<Base64-Image-Removed>) How Buying OEM Parts Can Save You Time and Money Why buy the real thing? OEM stands for Original Equipment Manufacturer. For model number WRS555SIHB00 ![PartSelect logo](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/images/ps-logo-mobile.svg) Hello E, thank you for your inquiry. Part: . Part Number: PS11738056. Category: refrigerator. Type: valves. Brand: . Fixes: . Compatible models: . be your water inlet valve. The valve has two solenoids that control either the dispenser or the ice maker. Even if your dispenser is working the ice maker solenoid may not be and vise versa. The valve can be tested with a multi meter and should be reading between 500 - 1500 ohms of resistance. If that part is testing correctly then you should look into your control board being the issue. Leonard March 16, 2023 The top ice maker is making ice, but the bottom ice maker is not. We replaced the water filter with no success. Could the Water Inlet Valve be the culprit? For model number WRF767SDHZ00 ![PartSelect logo](https://partselectcom-gtcdcddbene3cpes.z01.azurefd.net/images/ps-logo-mobile.svg) Hi Leonard, thank you for reaching out. Based on our research, the issue is probably with the refrigerator water inlet valve, part number PS11753251. If the valve is working fine and water is reaching the ice maker then you may need to replace the ice maker, part number PS16730670. We hope this sorts out your problem! 1 person found this helpful. samples.response:
Here is information for a Refrigerator Water Inlet Valve:

- **Part Number:** PS7784018
- **Price:** $62.60
- **Availability:** In Stock
- **Fixes:** Leaking, ice maker not making ice, not dispensing water, ice maker won't dispense ice, ice maker dispenses too little ice, leaks water.
- **Compatible Models:** AP5671757, 2692163, 242252702, 240321801, 240505101, 240505102, 240508101, 240508102, 240531101, 241734301.
- This valve is manufactured by Frigidaire for Frigidaire, Kenmore, Electrolux, and Crosley.
samples.reference: The water inlet valve is an electrically controlled valve that supplies water to the ice maker and water dispenser. It's replaced when the dispenser or ice maker stops getting water.
